title: Fundamental Determinants of Growth: The excess return of a firm is calculated as the...
summary: Calculate excess return as the difference between ROE and the cost of equity.
prompt: The excess return of a firm is calculated as the difference between its return on equity (ROE) and the cost of equity.
solution: Excess return represents the additional return that a firm generates over its cost of equity. It is calculated as ROE minus the cost of equity, indicating how well a firm is performing relative to its expected return.
